entities.name: SOCIAL CAPITAL
entities.description: The ability to develop and use social networks and their resources, serving as a means to exit poverty.

entities.name: SOCIAL NETWORKS
entities.description: The resources and connections that individuals develop and utilize within social capital.

entities.name: FARMING COMMUNITY
entities.description: The group of agricultural practitioners who adopt and benefit from improved agricultural technologies.

entities.name: IMPROVED AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Advanced agricultural tools and methods adopted by farming communities to improve productivity.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where ICRISAT conducted case studies on the role of social capital.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The research organization that conducted case studies on the uptake and diffusion of innovations.

entities.name: INNOVATIONS
entities.description: New agricultural developments whose successful uptake, diffusion, and impact are influenced by social capital.

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The socio-economic state from which social capital provides an exit path.

relationships.description: Social capital is defined as the ability to develop and use social networks
Social capital is central to understanding how the farming community adopts technologies
Social capital is crucial for the successful uptake, diffusion and impact of innovations
The farming community adopts and benefits from improved agricultural technologies
ICRISAT conducted case studies in India
Social capital provides an exit path from poverty
Improved agricultural technologies are a form of innovations
